Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows markedly elevated serum creatinine, low total CO₂, low serum protein and globulin, and significant proteinuria, glycosuria, and ketonuria. Urine is brown, cloudy, with low specific gravity. These findings suggest acute or chronic renal dysfunction, likely with nephrotic-range proteinuria, and possible metabolic derangement (e.g., diabetic ketoacidosis). Immediate clinical evaluation and nephrology consultation are recommended.
